entities.description: bookrag is a multi modal rag method evaluated for efficiency in terms of query time and token consumption
graph based rag methods are existing methods used as a baseline for comparing bookrag s efficiency
text based rag approaches are methods that generally exhibit lower latency and token usage due to the absence of vlm processing
vlm refers to vision language models  the processing component absent in purely text based rag approaches
docetl is a baseline method against which bookrag s token consumption and query latency are compared
mmlongbench is a dataset used to evaluate the token consumption of docetl and bookrag
figure 5 is a visual illustration showing the efficiency evaluation of bookrag in terms of query time and token consumption
53 million tokens is the amount of token consumption recorded for docetl on the mmlongbench dataset
5 million is the upper limit of token consumption required by bookrag on the mmlongbench dataset
2   represents the speedup factor achieved by bookrag compared to docetl in query latency
order of magnitude describes the scale of reduction in token consumption by bookrag compared to docetl

relations.description: bookrag maintains time and token costs comparable to existing graph based rag methods
bookrag maintains a balanced efficiency among multi modal methods compared to text based approaches which have lower latency
bookrag involves vlm processing for images  unlike purely text based rag approaches
bookrag reduces token consumption by an order of magnitude and achieves a speedup of up to 2x compared to docetl
docetl consumes over 53 million tokens on the mmlongbench dataset
bookrag requires less than 5 million tokens on the mmlongbench dataset
figure 5 illustrates the efficiency evaluation of bookrag
docetl consumes 53 million tokens on the mmlongbench dataset
bookrag requires less than 5 million tokens on the mmlongbench dataset
bookrag achieves a speedup of up to 2   compared to docetl
bookrag reduces token consumption by an order of magnitude compared to docetl
